reasoning: The 6-month treasury bill rate (DTB6) closed at 3.50 on Dec 19, which will serve as our baseline for Dec 21. Following the Fed's recent rate cut to 3.50%–3.75%, the trend in DTB6 has been downward to sideways, having dropped from 3.66 in November to 3.50 in mid-December. Short-term forecast models suggest a ~30-45% chance of the rate increasing above 3.50 by Dec 28, as markets generally expect mild downward drift in line with expectations of further rate cuts in 2026. For the later dates, while structural forces might usually push rates down if rate cuts occur, the explicit prompt constraint requires uncertainty to increase with forecast horizon. Thus, probabilities for the successive dates will be scaled closer to 0.5.
